title: fix: return 400 for malformed audio URL instead of 500
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Polite rejections for bad audio URLs
gist.blurb: We fixed a bug where malformed audio URLs would cause a server error. Now, you'll get a helpful 400 error instead, making debugging your API calls a breeze.
gist.summary: Fixed an issue where malformed percent-encoding in audio URLs caused a 500 server error instead of a 400 client error.
gist.impact: Developers will now receive a clear 400 Bad Request error when sending improperly encoded audio URLs, making debugging easier.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Nomnom creature. Nomnom is trying to eat a jagged, broken URL string (malformed percent-encoding) and looking confused, while Polly holds up a polite '400' stop sign. Cozy pixel art.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round nomnom creature looking confused while trying to eat a jagged, broken URL string. The cute bee mascot hovers nearby holding a small polite '400' stop sign. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like A Short Hike. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
